title: Scheduler for amp architecture with closed loop performance controller
abstract: Systems and methods are disclosed for scheduling threads on a processor that has at least two different core types, such as an asymmetric multiprocessing system. Each core type can run at a plurality of selectable voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) states. Threads from a plurality of processes can be grouped into thread groups. Execution metrics are accumulated for threads of a thread group and fed into a plurality of tunable controllers for the thread group. A closed loop performance control (CLPC) system determines a control effort for the thread group and maps the control effort to a recommended core type and DVFS state. A closed loop thermal and power management system can limit the control effort determined by the CLPC for a thread group, and limit the power, core type, and DVFS states for the system. Deferred interrupts can be used to increase performance.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A computer- implemented method of running threads on a system comprising a processor having a first cluster of one or more cores and a second cluster of one or more cores, the method comprising: grouping threads of one or more processes into one or more thread groups; running a first thread of a first thread group on a core of the first cluster of cores; incorporating a plurality of thread execution metrics of the first thread with a plurality of thread execution metrics of the first thread group; determining a first control effort for the first thread group based at least in part upon a plurality of thread group metrics associated with the first thread group; and recommending the first cluster or the second cluster for executing threads of the first thread group and determining a dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) setting for the recommended cluster. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein recommending the first cluster or second cluster, and determining a DVFS state setting for the recommended cluster comprises mapping the first control effort to a performance map of the cluster and DVFS states characterized by control effort. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein: the processor comprises an asymmetric processor; the first cluster comprises a plurality of performance cores (P-cores); and the second cluster comprises a plurality of efficient cores (E-cores). 4. The method of claim 1, further comprising: determining a second control effort for a second thread group, based at least in part upon a plurality of thread group metrics associated with the second thread group, wherein determining the second control effort is performed in response to determining that one or more threads of the second thread group were active on one or more cores, indicating that the second thread group was an active thread group; and 

 determining a second recommended cluster and DVFS state to execute threads of the second thread group, based at least in part on the second control effort. 5. The method of claim 4, further comprising: in response to determining that the first recommended cluster for the first thread group and the second recommended cluster for the second thread group are the same cluster: computing a maximum DVFS state as the maximum of the first recommended DVFS state and the second recommended DVFS state; setting the DVFS state for the cluster recommended for both the first and second thread groups to the computed maximum DVFS state; in response to determining that the first recommended cluster for the first thread group and the second recommended cluster for the second thread group are different clusters of cores: setting the DVFS state for the first recommended cluster to the first recommended DVFS state; and setting the DVFS state for the second recommended cluster to the second recommended DVFS state. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of thread execution metrics is measured at a periodic interval, the metrics comprising one or more of: a runnable time for a thread; a runnable time for a thread that adopted the first thread group from a voucher, and the runnable time for the thread is attributed to the first thread group execution metrics as of the time the thread adopted the first thread group from the voucher; core utilization time for the thread; and a number of input/output transactions performed by the thread, wherein the plurality of thread execution metrics is measured in response to an asynchronous event, the metrics comprising a scheduling latency for a thread or a work interval utilization. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of thread execution metrics provides an input to a tunable controller and the control effort for a thread group is determined from a combined output of each of the tunable controllers of metrics for the thread group. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein recommending the first cluster or the second cluster and determining a DVFS state setting for the recommended cluster comprises mapping the first 

 control effort to a performance map of the cluster and DVFS states characterized by control effort, wherein the control effort mapping to the performance map indicates that threads for the first thread group are eligible to run on the first cluster or the second cluster, and the recommendation for the first thread group to run on the first cluster or the second cluster is determined at least in part by a type of work that is performed by the first thread group. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the DVFS state recommendation for the first thread group is determined, at least in part, on a duration of time that the first thread group has be resident in a particular DVFS state. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the processor comprises an asymmetric processor, the first cluster comprises a plurality of performance cores (P-cores), the second cluster comprises a plurality of efficient cores (E-cores), and the method further comprises: determining that one or more threads of the first group are enqueued to run on the P-cores of the processor; determining that no P-cores of the processor are idle; determining that one or more E-cores are idle or are going idle; executing a thread of the first group on an E-core; and increasing the DVFS state of the E-cores by increasing at least one of frequency or voltage to the E-cores synchronously with the running of the thread of the first group on the E core. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising: determining that a thread in the first group is recommended for a P-core, but is running on an E-core; and running the thread on a P-core, in response to detecting that a P-core is idle or is going idle. 12. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having executable instructions stored thereon, that when executed by a processing system comprising at least one hardware processor coupled to a memory perform operations, the operations comprising a method as in any one of claims 1-11. 13. A system comprising at least one hardware processor coupled to a memory programmed with executable instructions that, when executed by the at least one hardware processor perform operations, the operations comprising: grouping threads of one or more processes into one or more thread groups; running a first thread of a first thread group on a core of a first cluster of cores; incorporating a plurality of thread execution metrics of the first thread with a plurality of thread execution metrics of the first thread group; determining a first control effort for the first thread group based at least in part upon a plurality of thread group metrics associated with the first thread group; and recommending the first or second cluster of cores for executing threads of the first thread group and determining a dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) setting for the recommended cluster of cores. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein recommending the first cluster of cores or the second cluster of cores and determining a DVFS state setting for the recommended cluster of cores comprises mapping the first control effort to a performance map of the cluster of cores and DVFS states characterized by control effort. 15. The system of claim 13, wherein: the processor comprises an asymmetric processor; the first cluster of cores comprises a plurality of performance cores (P-cores); and the second cluster of cores comprises a plurality of efficient cores (E-cores). 16. The system of claim 13, the operations further comprising: determining a second control effort for a second thread group, based at least in part upon a plurality of thread group metrics associated with the second thread group, wherein determining the second control effort is performed in response to determining that one or more threads of the second thread group were active on one or more cores, indicating that the second thread group was an active thread group; and determining a second recommended cluster of cores and DVFS state to execute threads of the second thread group, based at least in part on the second control effort. 17. The system of claim 16, the operations further comprising: 

 in response to determining that the first recommended cluster of cores for the first thread group and the second recommended cluster of cores for the second thread group are the same cluster of cores: computing a maximum DVFS state as the maximum of the first recommended DVFS state and the second recommended DVFS state; setting the DVFS state for the cluster of cores recommended for both the first and second thread groups to the computed maximum DVFS state; in response to determining that the first recommended cluster of cores for the first thread group and the second recommended cluster of cores for the second thread group are different cluster of cores: setting the DVFS state for the first recommended cluster of cores to the first recommended DVFS state; and setting the DVFS state for the second recommended cluster of cores to the second recommended DVFS state. 18. The system of claim 13, wherein the plurality of thread execution metrics is measured at a periodic interval, the metrics comprising one or more of: a runnable time for a thread; a runnable time for a thread that adopted the first thread group from a voucher, and the runnable time for the thread is attributed to the first thread group execution metrics as of the time the thread adopted the first thread group from the voucher; core utilization time for the thread; or a number of input/output transactions performed by the thread; or the plurality of thread execution metrics are measured in response to an asynchronous event, the metrics comprising one or more of: a scheduling latency for a thread; or a work interval utilization. 19. The system of claim 13, wherein each of the plurality of thread execution metrics provides an input to a tunable controller and the control effort for a thread group is determined from a combined output of each of the tunable controllers of metrics for the thread group. 20. The system of claim 13, wherein recommending the first cluster of cores or the second cluster of cores and determining a DVFS state setting for the recommended cluster of cores 

 comprises mapping the first control effort to a performance map of cluster of cores and DVFS states characterized by control effort and wherein the control effort mapping to the performance map indicates that threads for the first thread group are eligible to run on the first cluster of cores or the second cluster of cores, and the recommendation for the first thread group to run on the first cluster, or second cluster, or cores is determined at least in part by a type of work that is performed by the first thread group. 21. The system of claim 20, wherein the DVFS state recommendation for the first thread group is determined, at least in part, on a duration of time that the first thread group has be resident in a particular DVFS state. 22. The system of claim 13, wherein the processor comprises an asymmetric processor, the first cluster of cores comprises a plurality of performance cores (P-cores), the second cluster of cores comprises a plurality of efficient cores (E-cores), and the operations further comprise: determining that one or more threads of the first group are enqueued to run on the P-cores of the processor; determining that no P-cores of the processor are idle; determining that one or more E-cores are idle or are going idle; executing a thread of the first group on an E-core; and increasing the DVFS state of the E-cores by increasing at least one of frequency or voltage to the E-cores synchronously with the running of the thread of the first group on the E core. 23. The system of claim 22, the operations further comprising: determining that a thread in the first group is recommended for a P-core, but is running on an E-core; and running the thread on a P-core, in response to detecting that a P-core is idle or is going idle.
